---
title: CV
format:
  quarto-cv-pdf: default
author: Adrián Zancajo
date: last-modified
phone:
address: "Edifici E · Campus UAB · Cerdanyola del Vallès 08193, Spain"
email: adrian.zancajo@gmail.com
fontsize: 11pt
#fontfamily: ebgaramond-maths # default font LaTeX package https://tug.org/FontCatalogue/about.html
#mainfont: Ubuntu # if you want to use a different locally installed font
#CJKmainfont: Noto Sans CJK JP # locally installed Asian character font, must use with xelatex
#github: github # github account
orcid: 0000-0002-4431-8155 # orcid identifier
#osf: ymqui # osf five character id
twitter: AdrianZancajo
web: azancajo.github.com
fontawesome: yes
nocite: '@*'
bibliography: 
  peer: articles.bib
  books: books.bib
  reports: reports_and_working_papers.bib
validate-yaml: false
csl: apa-cv.csl
---

# Education

PhD in Sociology, *Autonomous University of Barcelona* \hfill 2017

M.Sc. in Education Research, *Autonomous University of Barcelona* \hfill 2012

B.A. in Economics, *Autonomous University of Barcelona* \hfill 2009

# Academic positions

Jan 2019 - Present: \hfill British Academy Postdoctoral Fellow. School of Education, University of Glasgow.

### Oct 2017 - Dec 2018:
**MOVE-IN Louvain Postdoctoral Fellow.**  
GIRSEF, Catholic University of Louvain.

### Feb 2013 - Aug 2017:
**Research Fellow (Education Policy and International Development).**  
Department of Education Policy and International Development.

# Publications

## Articles in peer review journals

::: {#refs-peer}
:::

## Books and book chapters

::: {#refs-books}
:::

# Policy reports and working papers

::: {#refs-reports}
:::

# Research projects and contracts

## Competitive Calls

1. **Is it Possible to Decommodify Education? Evidence and Lessons from the Chilean Education Market Reform.**  
   Funding source: British Academy  
   Principal investigator: Dr Adrian Zancajo  
   Starting date and duration: 01/01/2019, 3.5 years  
   Amount: £305,310  
   Ref.: PF2\180006

2. **Governing the educational and labour market trajectories of secondary TVET graduates in Chile.**  
   Funding source: Newton RCUK  
   Principal investigator: Dr Oscar Valiente  
   Starting date and duration: 01/02/2016, 3 years  
   Amount: £206,698  
   Ref.: ES/N019229/1

3. **Public-Private Partnerships in Educational Governance: An analysis of its dissemination, implementation and impact in a globalizing world – EDUPARTNER.**  
   Funding source: European Commission  
   Call FP7-PEOPLE-2011-CIG  
   Principal investigator: Professor Antoni Verger  
   Starting date and duration: 01/02/2013, 4 years  
   Amount: £86,039  
   Ref.: GA-2012-322350

4. **The new quasi-market reforms in education in Latin America. An analysis of policy processes and policy impacts on inequalities and poverty.**  
   Funding source: Spanish Ministry of Science  
   Principal investigators: Professor Xavier Bonal and Professor Antoni Verger  
   Starting date and duration: 01/01/2012, 3 years  
   Amount: £43,020  
   Ref.: CSO2011-22697

## Most Relevant Research Contracts

1. **Regulating Public-Private Partnerships, governing non-state schools: An equity perspective.**  
   Principal investigator: Dr Adrian Zancajo (University of Glasgow)  
   Funding institution: UNESCO  
   Starting date and duration: 15/05/2020, 6 months  
   Amount: £7,883

2. **Equivalence criteria in Barcelona’s educational planning: diagnosis and proposals.**  
   Principal investigators: Professor Xavier Bonal and Dr Adrian Zancajo  
   Funding institution: Consorci d’Educació de Barcelona  
   Starting date and duration: 01/05/2017, 1 year  
   Amount: £22,397

3. **The political economy of education privatization. A systematic review.**  
   Principal investigator: Professor Antoni Verger  
   Funding institution: Education International  
   Starting date and duration: 10/01/2015, 1 year  
   Amount: £19,812

4. **Formulating Potential Indicators for a Broad and Bold Global Education Agenda for Post-2015.**  
   Principal investigators: Professor Antoni Verger, Professor Xavier Bonal, and Adrian Zancajo  
   Funding institution: Open Society Foundations (OSF)  
   Length: 21/05/2015, 9 months  
   Amount: £7,235

5. **PISA 2012 in Catalonia.**  
   Principal investigator: Professor Xavier Bonal  
   Funding institution: Fundació Jaume Bofill  
   Length: 18/01/2014, 1 year  
   Amount: £19,381

6. **Educational Inequalities and public-private partnerships in Colombian Education.**  
   Principal investigators: Professor Antoni Verger and Professor Xavier Bonal  
   Funding institution: Open Society Foundations (OSF)  
   Length: 21/05/2015, 9 months  
   Amount: £15,936

7. **Catalan Youth Survey 2012- Education.**  
   Principal investigators: Adrian Zancajo and Alba Castejón  
   Funding institution: Observatori Català de la Joventut  
   Length: 12/09/2012, 6 months  
   Amount: £2,584

8. **TVET and Employment in Catalonia.**  
   Principal investigator: Dr Oscar Valiente  
   Funding institution: Fundació Jaume Bofill  
   Length: 10/03/2012, 9 months  
   Amount: £21,535

9. **The measure of educational development.**  
   Principal investigators: Professor Xavier Bonal and Professor Antoni Verger  
   Funding institution: International Institute for Educational Planning – UNESCO  
   Length: 05/09/2011, 5 months  
   Amount: £7,752

10. **PISA – 2009 in Catalonia.**  
    Principal investigator: Professor Ferran Ferrer  
    Funding institution: Fundació Jaume Bofill  
    Length: 12/01/2012, 9 months  
    Amount: £17,228
    
# References

Available upon request, though likely would include Karl Pearson and Ronald A. Fisher.
